ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Suspend Powers Of Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-21-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Thirkell for Joseph Neri (Trustee). Charles Wolff for Petitioners.

Mr. Wolff reported that they mediated and reached a settlement which is going to be presented to the court for approval, and addressed issues raised in the examiner's notes. Mr. Thirkell stated that the he did not represent Joseph in a personal capacity, and that the January 2nd removal petition should be dropped. The Court stated that the petition for approval of the settlement agreement should be heard on January 29, 2008, that it did not have a problem with Mr. Wild being the Guardian Ad Litem, and that all orders are to be submitted by Counsel. The January 2nd hearing is continued to January 29, 2008. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR #11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lewis.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Suspend Powers Of Trustee
Off Calendar Nov-28-2007 Continued To Dec-19-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Julia Lingys and Edward Thirkell for and with Joseph Neri (Objector). Charles Wolff for Leanne Tintori Wells, Susan Tintori Marciano and Barbara Tintori (Petitioners).

Mr. Thirkell stated that his client complied with the Court's order and introduced a copy of an e-mail dated November 13th as evidence which was marked and identified as respondents exhibit 1. Mr. Wolff objected stating that this was not an evidentiary hearing. Mr. Thirkell stated that he received the Supplemental Declaration and the Memorandum of Points of Authority yesterday and that the IRS documents were delivered yesterday. Mr. Thirkell expressed concern about his client being in New York when the ex parte matter was calendared stating that he had no chance to seek counsel, and stated that Mr. Wolff was not willing to mediate or reach global settlement. In addition, he reported that his client received a check from Cushman Rexrode and asked for instructions from the Court as to how to deposit the funds due to the fact that the stipulation blocked his client from doing so. Mr. Wolff argued that Mr. Neri should be removed as trustee stating that there was a conflict of interest pursuant to Code 16005. Mr Thirkell requested an evidentiary hearing, and sees no reason for Mr. Neri being removed stating that the assets are protected by the Court Order. The Court continued the matter to December 19, 2007 for further status at which point the Court will reconsider appointing an independent trustee. The Stipulation will be amended to allow the funds to be deposited into a 3 month CD. If Mr. Neri remains as trustee, the Court will authorize hiring counsel for the trust regarding the IRS audit only, not for personal representation. The exhibit was returned to Mr. Thirkell.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lee and Deputy Smith.

Ex Parte Application To Suspend Trustee'S Powers And Appoint Temporary Co-Trustees
Off Calendar Nov-09-2007 Off Calendar
